# Pieter's Cloud Journey

> Weekly fragments on AI, Azure, Kubernetes, open source, and community by Pieter de Bruin. Mostly notes to self. May be useful to you too.

Pieter is a technologist at Microsoft working in the learning and developer content space. He writes short weekly blog posts — typically 80–200 words — that curate and comment on developments in cloud technology, AI, and open source. The blog has been active since 2020 with 157 posts.

## Topics

- Artificial intelligence: AI agents, MCP (Model Context Protocol), RAG, GitHub Copilot, Copilot Studio, AI Foundry
- Azure cloud: AKS (Kubernetes), identity, cost management, functions, architecture
- Open source: contributing, community events, .NET, Linux
- Learning and community: Microsoft Learn, mentoring, personal productivity, public speaking, volunteering

## Key Posts

- [How we built Learn MCP Server](https://blog.pdebruin.org/how-we-built-learn-mcp-server/): How the Microsoft Learn team built an MCP Server that connects AI agents to trusted documentation
- [MCP for beginners](https://blog.pdebruin.org/mcp-for-beginners/): Introduction to Model Context Protocol
- [Learn MCP Server generally available](https://blog.pdebruin.org/learn-mcp-server-generally-available/): GA announcement of Learn MCP Server
- [Expert generalist](https://blog.pdebruin.org/expert-generalist/): Reflections on being a generalist in technology
- [Mentoring](https://blog.pdebruin.org/mentoring/): Why mentoring matters and how to approach it
- [Volunteering at three local events](https://blog.pdebruin.org/volunteering-at-three-local-events/): Meeting real people at community events
- [Personal productivity](https://blog.pdebruin.org/personal-productivity/): Time management and prioritization
- [My setup](https://blog.pdebruin.org/my-setup/): Tools and workflow for cross-platform development
